emails.subject: W-2 Question
emails.body: W-2 Question Rita, I was recently quizzing payroll on why I received two W-2s (which sum in aggregate to my 12/31/01 payroll stub) when I thought had been employed by the same co. (366) all year. Her records indicated that you had entered a change during the year. Could you look into why I can listed as having worked for both Enron Operations Services Co Enron Corp. in 2001? Shelley Corman x37083

emails.subject: RE: W-2 Question
emails.body: Shelley, I have checked the system and find that the only changes made to your record during 2001 are as follows: 1/1/2001 4/15/2001 VP Reg Gov. Affairs Co. 0366 4/15/2001 12/16/2001 Title change to VP Gas Logistics no company change 12/17/2001 12/31/2001 FMLA Leave no company change 1/1/2002 to present company change to 179E. I have nothing indicating a company change for 2001. Let me know who in payroll you spoke with and maybe I can work with them to determine their conclusion. Rita To: Corman, Shelley

emails.subject: RE: W-2 Question
emails.body: Unfortunately I didn't write down the name of the person in payroll that returned my call to the main payroll number. Whoever it was logged into their system and said that the record showed you making the co. change. Could you give payroll a call? To: Corman, Shelley

emails.subject: RE: W-2 Question
emails.body: I will make the call and research. Rita To: Houston, Rita
